searchDocs.title: Get​
searchDocs.content: </> Preload.Get() → {[string]: Sound }-- Dictionary of all Preloaded Sounds. Gets a dictionary of all Preloaded Sounds including entries added after startup. print(VluxySF.Preload.Get())

searchDocs.title: Selected​
searchDocs.content: </> Preload.Selected( ...: string | Sound | Types.SoundDefinition-- Varargs to Preload. ) → () Preloads the provided variables and stores them internally. INFO If a Sound is Preloaded Warming it will have no effect. Provided Sounds in Varargs are Cloned and used internally. local sound1 = ... local sound2 = ... local sound3 = ... local soundDefinition1 = ... local soundDefinition2 = ... local soundName1 = "MySound1" local soundName2 = "MySound2" Preload.Selected( sound1, sound2, sound3, soundDefinition1, soundDefinition2, soundName1, soundName2 )

searchDocs.title: Using VluxySF
searchDocs.content: Using VluxySF Here are some quick links to get started using VluxySF Installation guideAPI DocsInitializationFolder setupExamplesAdviceExtension

searchDocs.title: Why this library exists?​
searchDocs.content: This library is designed for fast iteration and small to medium sized games!    I made it because I didnt want to have to search through folders to find the sound I wanted. I would have to write multiple lines just to go fetch the sound I wanted and I found it anoying. On top of that if the sound location changed or the sound didnt exist anymore the entire block would error. So, of course i spend over 100 hours trying to fix that with a library as thats completly logical!!!  This can also work in larger games without much issue the only bottleneck is that it becomes less convenient to use after a cetain amount of sounds. And thats because there is no heiarchy when fetching sounds; its completly flat. So, the difficulty of naming a sound with a unique name scales up as the amount of sounds scale up.  For the Extension there is a limit of 800 sounds as that should be a safe number for a Language Server to not error. But, the library itself should be able to handle a few thousand sounds no problem if you don't care for the Extension.

searchDocs.title: Library Setup​
searchDocs.content: You should adjust your sounds in studios EditMode for your most common use case. Then use presets or manually adjust your sounds if you need to change small things about them at runtime. If a sounds properties are too diffrent from the original copy then it may be better to just make a new Sound even if it has the same SoundID. Sounds should not determine your Buisness Logic atleast 95% of the time. Sounds should go ontop of your code not mold it.

searchDocs.title: Memory Optimizations​
searchDocs.content: Optimizations may be important if you need your game to run on cellular devices or old devices.

searchDocs.title: Optimization List​
searchDocs.content: Use 3rdParty Software like Audacity or FL Studio to compress the audio size. Only Preload sounds that you know needs it. Lazy Load your sounds wherever possible. Delete Sounds when you know you dont need them for the moment.

searchDocs.title: Sound Editing​
searchDocs.content: Sound editing should be a last resort as that that is the most tedious of the methods. Also there is a monthly import limit in roblox for audio. But this is the best memory saving method if its worth it to you.

searchDocs.title: Pre Loading​
searchDocs.content: This library makes preloading easy as you just need to tag with VluxySF_Preload. But just because its easy that doesn't mean you should preload everything. Especially if your game is going to be using more than 20 sounds. The more Sounds you preload the more memory will be taken up throught the game. There is no way to revert Preloading a Sound.

searchDocs.title: Lazy Loading​
searchDocs.content: Lazy loading is when you only create an object when its needed. This library makes that easy as you just need to call VluxySF.Fetch.  Just make sure that you call that function right when you need the sound and there you go!  This will only work if the soundID was not preloaded and there is no other sounds with the same soundID!

searchDocs.title: Deleting Your Sound​
searchDocs.content: If you want to save more memory then just delete the sound made by the VluxySF library once your done playing it. By using VluxySF.FetchPlayNow it will automaticly do this for you.  This will only work if the soundID was not preloaded and there is no other sounds with the same soundID!

searchDocs.title: When to optimize?​
searchDocs.content: It may depend on the Project but in most cases if your codebase is designed well or well enough it can be done later down the line.  For instance, maybe your just polishing your game up. Well that could be the best time to start optimizing your sound memory if its needed.  But of course only optimize when you need too and if you need too!

searchDocs.title: ToDefinition​
searchDocs.content: </> Conversions.ToDefinition( sound: Sound -- The Sound to convert. ) → SoundDefinition-- The resulting SoundDefinition. Converts a Sound into a SoundDefinition. local mySound = ... local soundDefinition = VluxySF.Conversions.ToDefinition(mySound) print(soundDefinition)

searchDocs.title: FromDefinition​
searchDocs.content: </> Conversions.FromDefinition( definition: SoundDefinition-- The SoundDefinition to convert. ) → Sound -- The resulting Sound. Converts a SoundDefinition into a Sound. local soundDefinition = { Properties = { Name = "MySound", SoundId = "rbxassetid://123456789", Volume = 0.5, SoundGroup = "GAME", }, Effects = {}, EffectCount = 0 } local mySound = VluxySF.Conversions.FromDefinition(soundDefinition) mySound:Play()

searchDocs.title: Wally Configuration​
searchDocs.content: Once Wally is installed, run wally init on your project directory, and then add VluxySF as a dependency. For example, the following could be a wally.toml file for a project that includes a few of these modules:  [package] name = "your_name/your_project" version = "0.1.0" registry = "https://github.com/UpliftGames/wally-index" realm = "shared" [dependencies] VluxySF = "greenviper126/VluxySF@^1"   To install this dependency, run wally install within your project. Wally will create a Package folder in your directory with the installed dependency.

searchDocs.title: Rojo Configuration​
searchDocs.content: The Package folder created by Wally should be synced into Roblox Studio through your Rojo configuration. For instance, a Rojo configuration might have the following entry to sync the Packages folder into ReplicatedStorage:  { "name": "vluxysf", "tree": { "$className": "DataModel", "ReplicatedStorage": { "$className": "ReplicatedStorage", "Packages": { "$path": "Packages" } } } }

searchDocs.title: Get​
searchDocs.content: </> Cache.Get() → {[string]: Sound }-- The current sounds being Warmed. Returns a hash of the current sounds that are warmed. print(VluxySF.Cache.Get())

searchDocs.title: IsWarmed​
searchDocs.content: </> Cache.IsWarmed( soundName: string-- Name of the Sound. ) → boolean-- Is that sound warmed? Tells you if a sound from its name is currently warmed. print(VluxySF.Cache.IsWarmed("MySound"))

searchDocs.title: Warm​
searchDocs.content: </> Cache.Warm( soundName: string-- The name of the Sound. ) → () Loads the Sound to the Client. If the the same Sound is fetched it will instead Clone from it. WARNING You should only warm if you need a sound to play without a delay or to play without fail but dont plan to constantly use it. TIP You can Warm at the top of a script or a seperate script if the sound you play is in a connection or function that gets called later. Some examples include loading a map and its sounds in. Maybe warming the sounds when first loading the map is a good idea!? VluxySF.Cache.Warm("MySound") --now if its used later on it should be ready to play. --do stuff task.wait(5) --do stuff VluxySF.FetchplayOnce("MySound") --should play without delay now

searchDocs.title: Chill​
searchDocs.content: </> Cache.Chill( soundName: string-- The name of the Sound. ) → () If a Sound with that name is currently warmed it is removed it from the cache. INFO Removing a sound from the cache does not garentee that it will be unloaded. If you still have Sounds with the same Id it will still be in memmory. VluxySF.Cache.Warm("MySound") local onPlay = Instance.new("BindableEvent") local myWarmedSound = ... local function playSound() myWarmedSound:Play() --plays without delay end OnPlay.Event:Connect(playSound) OnPlay.Destroying:Connect(function() myWarmedSound:Destroy() --clean up our sound VluxySF.Cache.Chill("MySound") -- now the sound isn't warmed end) --Now if OnPlay is destroyed it wont be warmed anymore. --So, if the soundId isnt used anywhere else it will be removed from memmory.

searchDocs.title: Get​
searchDocs.content: </> SoundGroups.Get() → SoundGroups-- Dictionary of SoundGroups. Returns the SoundGroups registerd in the Schema. print(VluxySF.Groups.Get())

searchDocs.title: GetByName​
searchDocs.content: </> SoundGroups.GetByName( name: string-- Name of the SoundGroup. ) → SoundGroup? -- SoundGroup if it exists. Finds a SoundGroup by name. local gameSoundGroup = VluxySF.Groups.GetByName("GAME") if gameSoundGroup then gameSoundGroup.Volume = 0.3 end

searchDocs.title: GetNames​
searchDocs.content: </> SoundGroups.GetNames() → {string}-- Array of SoundGroup names. Gets an arrray of all SoundGroup names within the main group. print(VluxySF.Groups.GetNames())

searchDocs.title: SetMainVolume​
searchDocs.content: </> SoundGroups.SetMainVolume( mainVolume: number-- Volume level to set. ) → () Sets the volume of the main SoundGroup that all other groups derive from. VluxySF.Groups.SetMainVolume(0.3)

searchDocs.title: SetVolumes​
searchDocs.content: </> SoundGroups.SetVolumes( mainVolume: number?,-- -- Main volume level to set. volumes: {[string]: number}-- Dictionary of the volumes to adjust. ) → () Sets Volumes to the provided levels. TIP MainVolume and the individual volume elements are optional when using SoundGroups.SetVolumes. local mainVolume = 1 local volumes = { ["GAME"] = 0.7, ["UI"] = 0.5, } VluxySF.Groups.SetVolumes(mainVolume, volumes)

searchDocs.title: Instructions​
searchDocs.content: Install the Extension in VS-Code and install the Plugin in Roblox.  Make sure your ports match in the extension and plugin; by default its 7842.  Once done just click on the VluxySF icon in the plugins tab and you should hear a connect sound. If it stays connected then you should be ready to bake your types.

searchDocs.title: How It Works​
searchDocs.content: What this companion software will do is bake types for you. More specifically it will make a unioned string literal of all your sound names under the SOUNDS Configuration.  It is highly recommend that you use Luau-LSP for your Language Server Provider.  If you do end up using Luau-LSP then make sure Enable Fragment AutoComplete is off. At the moment(2/28/2026) it causes inline suggests to not auto popup most the time.    Example of the extension in use:  Note: This extension has a limit of 800 sounds for type baking!    Example of what the type will look like internally by default:  --!strict export type BakedTypes = string return nil   Example of what the type will look like internally once baked:  --!strict export type BakedTypes = "A Cure for All" | "Agaiiiiiin" | "Bubble Pop Parade" | "Clara OL" | ... return nil   Example of what the type will look like internally once baked if LooseTypes is on in the Extension:  --!strict export type BakedTypes = string | "A Cure for All" | "Agaiiiiiin" | "Bubble Pop Parade" | "Clara OL" | ... return nil

searchDocs.title: SoundGroups​
searchDocs.content: </> type SoundGroups = {[string]: SoundGroup } Dictionary of SoundGroups from the Schema.

searchDocs.title: SoundDefinitionInstanced​
searchDocs.content: </> type SoundDefinitionInstanced = Sound & SoundChildrenInstanced A Sound with SoundEffects parented.

searchDocs.title: SoundChildrenInstanced​
searchDocs.content: </> interface SoundChildrenInstanced { TremoloSoundEffect: TremoloSoundEffect? ReverbSoundEffect: ReverbSoundEffect? PitchShiftSoundEffect: PitchShiftSoundEffect? FlangeSoundEffect: FlangeSoundEffect? EqualizerSoundEffect: EqualizerSoundEffect? EchoSoundEffect: EchoSoundEffect? DistortionSoundEffect: DistortionSoundEffect? CompressorSoundEffect: CompressorSoundEffect? ChorusSoundEffect: ChorusSoundEffect? } All possible SoundEffects to find within a Sound.

searchDocs.title: The Basics: Folder Structure​
searchDocs.content: VluxySF uses your folder structure to automatically group and identify sounds.  You do not need to manually assign SoundGroups or create them!    Legend:  ⚙️ = Configuration Instance 📁 = Folder Instance 🔊 = Sound Instance   Naming Conventions:  UPPER_CASE for SoundGroupsPascalCase for Folders is recommended but most naming convention will workPascalCase or camelCase for Sounds is recommended but most naming convention will workSound names are used as keys for programmatic access (e.g., VluxySF.Fetch("explosion1")).    Example:  SOUNDS⚙️ ← Base file ├─ MUSIC⚙️ ← Name of SoundGroup │ ├─ mainTheme🔊 ← Grouped to MUSIC SoundGroup │ └─ battle🔊 ← Grouped to MUSIC SoundGroup └─ SFX⚙️ ← Name of SoundGroup ├─ click🔊 ← Grouped to SFX SoundGroup └─ explosion🔊 ← Grouped to SFX SoundGroup   Music and SFX become SoundGroups, and their children are grouped accordingly.  Tip: Once SoundGroup Configurations are defined (MUSIC, SFX, etc.), organization within them is your choice.

searchDocs.title: Organizing with Subfolders​
searchDocs.content: You can use additional folders (📁) inside SoundGroups to keep your sounds organized.  Example:  SOUNDS⚙️ └─ SFX⚙️ ├─ UI📁 │ └─ click🔊 └─ Game📁 └─ explosion🔊   Tip: Organize your folders however you want! The heiarchy does not effect the library. Note: All Sounds need to have unique names. A warning will appear at runtime if you go against this.

searchDocs.title: Preloading Sounds Automatically​
searchDocs.content: Want certain sounds to be ready instantly? You can tag any Folder or Sound with VluxySF_Preload and it will automaticly preload once the client is started.  How to use:  SOUNDS⚙️ ├─ MUSIC⚙️ │ ├─ mainTheme🔊 │ └─ battles📁 <-- you can tag with "VluxySF_Preload" to preload sounds within this folder ├─ importantSound1🔊 └─ importantSound2🔊 └─ SFX⚙️ ├─ click🔊 ├─ explosions📁 ├─ CommonExplosionSound🔊 <-- You can tag with "VluxySF_Preload" to preload this sound └─ UncommonLongExplosionSound🔊

searchDocs.title: Preload Timeout​
searchDocs.content: You can set an optional timeout (in seconds) for preloading. If preloading takes too long, it will continue in the background. Default is 5 seconds.  --!strict local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local VluxySF = require(ReplicatedStorage.Packages.VluxySF) local preloadTimeout = 10 VluxySF._initClient(preloadTimeout) -- <-- reaches this line once sounds are preloaded or timeout is reached

searchDocs.title: Configuring Sound Instances​
searchDocs.content: Each Sound instance can be customized with any Roblox sound properties and child sound effects except for the Parent:  Properties: Set properties like SoundId, Volume, PlaybackSpeed, etc, directly on the Sound instance.Effects: Add child instances such as EqualizerSoundEffect, ReverbSoundEffect, etc, to the Sound Instance.  Note: Any Instances parented to a Sound thats not a SoundEffect will not be serialized. Note: Only 1 of each Class will be serialized.

searchDocs.title: End Result​
searchDocs.content: A SOUNDS Configuration should look something like this when you’re done in Roblox Studio:    Note: This should be inside ServerStorage. It is recommended to make it a direct child of that service.

searchDocs.title: Default​
searchDocs.content: </> Jitter.Default( center: number,-- The central value around which the jitter will occur. spread: number-- The maximum deviation from the center value. ) → () → number-- Generates the jitter value when called. Standard Jitter function that randomly varies from the center value +- the spread. local myJitter = Jitter.Default(1, 0.15) local sound1 = ... sound1.PlaybackSpeed = myJitter() -- Sets a random value. local sound2 = ... sound2.PlaybackSpeed = myJitter() -- Sets another random value.

searchDocs.title: Range​
searchDocs.content: </> Jitter.Range( min: number,-- The minimum value for the jitter range. max: number-- The maximum value for the jitter range. ) → () → number-- Generates the jitter value when called. Essentially just a random number that requires a specified range. local myJitter = Jitter.Range(0.85, 1.15) local sound1 = ... sound1.PlaybackSpeed = myJitter() -- Sets a random value. local sound2 = ... sound2.PlaybackSpeed = myJitter() -- Sets another random value.

searchDocs.title: Scale​
searchDocs.content: </> Jitter.Scale( spread: number-- The maximum deviation from the center value. ) → () → number-- Generates the jitter value when called. Very similar to Jitter.Default but the center value is 1. local myJitter = Jitter.Scale(0.15) local sound1 = ... sound1.PlaybackSpeed = myJitter() -- Sets a random value. local sound2 = ... sound2.PlaybackSpeed = myJitter() -- Sets another random value.

searchDocs.title: UnitLog​
searchDocs.content: </> Jitter.UnitLog( center: number,-- The central value around which the jitter will occur. spread: number-- The maximum deviation from the center value. ) → () → number-- Generates the jitter value when called. Produces jitter values with a shaped distribution that favors the center value. local myJitter = Jitter.UnitLog(1, 0.15) local sound1 = ... sound1.PlaybackSpeed = myJitter() -- Sets a random shaped value. local sound2 = ... sound2.PlaybackSpeed = myJitter() -- Sets another random shaped value. TIP This can be used to make more natural repetitious sounds like footsteps.

searchDocs.title: SoundSchema​
searchDocs.content: </> interface SoundSchema { SoundGroupContainer: SoundGroup -- Main SoundGroup for all other SoundGroups. SoundGroups: SoundGroups-- Dictionary of SoundGroups under the Main SoundGroup. UnattachedSounds: Configuration -- Configuration for Un-Parented Sounds. WarmedSounds: Configuration -- Configuration for Warmed Sounds. PreloadedSounds: Configuration -- Configuration for Preloaded Sounds. SoundDefinitions: SoundDefinitions-- Dictionary of SoundDefinitions. PreloadIds: {string}-- List of sound names to Preload. } The Schema for the library that is shared on client and server.

searchDocs.title: Get​
searchDocs.content: </> Schema.Get() → SoundSchema-- The internal sound schema used by the system. The full Schema used internally by the library. print(VluxySF.Schema.Get())

searchDocs.title: GetDefinitions​
searchDocs.content: </> Schema.GetDefinitions() → SoundDefinitions-- All converted sound data Returns all SoundDefinitions in the Schema. print(VluxySF.Schema.GetDefinitions())

searchDocs.title: GetDefinition​
searchDocs.content: </> Schema.GetDefinition( definitionName: string-- The name of the sound definition to retrieve. ) → SoundDefinition?-- The SoundDefinition associated with the provided name. Retrieves a SoundDefinition from the Schema based on its name. print(VluxySF.Schema.GetDefinition("MySound"))

searchDocs.title: GetNames​
searchDocs.content: </> Schema.GetNames() → {string}-- All registered sound names Returns all sound names in the library. print(VluxySF.Schema.GetNames())

searchDocs.title: Fetch​
searchDocs.content: RequiresSchema </> VluxySF.Fetch( name: string,-- The name of the Sound. parent: Instance? ,-- Parent of the sound. preset: (Sound ) → ()?-- Preset to apply. ) → Sound -- The fetched sound instance. Finds the specified SoundDefinition and gives back a Sound. If somthing goes wrong a Blank Sound is returned instead. INFO If a sound is Preloaded it is instead Cloned from the preloadFolder If a sound is Warmed it is instead Cloned from the warmed cache. WARNING If the sound is not preloaded or warmed it may take a moment to fetch and load as it needs to be created first. local mySound = VluxySF.Fetch("MySound") mySound.Volume = 0.3 local echoEffect = mySound.EchoSoundEffect if echoEffect then echoEffect.WetLevel = 1.3 end mySound:Play()

searchDocs.title: FetchStrict​
searchDocs.content: RequiresSchema </> VluxySF.FetchStrict( name: string,-- The name of the Sound. parent: Instance? ,-- Parent of the Sound. preset: (Sound ) → ()?-- Preset to apply. ) → Sound? -- The fetched Sound instance if success. Similar to VluxySF.Fetch but will not return a Blank Sound on fail. local mySound = VluxySF.Fetch("MySound") if mySound then mySound.Volume = 0.3 local echoEffect = mySound.EchoSoundEffect if echoEffect then echoEffect.WetLevel = 1.3 end mySound:Play() end

searchDocs.title: FetchPlayOnce​
searchDocs.content: RequiresSchema </> VluxySF.FetchPlayOnce( name: string,-- The name of the Sound. parent: Instance? ,-- Parent of the Sound. preset: (Sound ) → ()?,-- Preset to apply. waitOnLoad: boolean?-- Wait for the Sound to load? Always separate thread. ) → () → ()-- Cleanup function. Plays a OneShot Sound and cleans up after playing. VluxySF.FetchPlayOnce("MySound")

searchDocs.title: 1. Setting Up the Sound Folder​
searchDocs.content: First, create a Configuration named SOUNDS and put it in ServerStorage. This should containing all your sound instances that you want to use for this library. For details on organizing this folder, see Folder Setup.  It is required that you do this or unexpected behavior may occur!

searchDocs.title: 2. Server Initialization​
searchDocs.content: On the server, inject the Configuration containing your instanced sounds:  local ServerStorage = game:GetService("ServerStorage") local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local VluxySF = require(ReplicatedStorage.Packages.VluxySF) -- Reference to the folder/config of the actual sounds you want to use local soundFolder = ServerStorage:FindFirstChild("SOUNDS") -- Initialize the server VluxySF.Startup.InitServer(soundFolder) -- <--schema can be used here

searchDocs.title: 3. Client Initialization​
searchDocs.content: On the client its a very similar setup:  local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local VluxySF = require(ReplicatedStorage.Packages.VluxySF) -- Initialize the client VluxySF.Startup.InitClient() -- <--schema can be used here   For initializing the client you can also provide a timeout for any sounds you tagged for preloading

searchDocs.title: 4. Basic Use​
searchDocs.content: In order to fully use this library in other modules you need to wait for the schema:   local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local VluxySF = require(ReplicatedStorage.Packages.VluxySF) -- wait for schema to be ready VluxySF.Startup.WaitForSchema() -- <--schema can be used here   If you know with 100% certaintly that the schema is loaded you do not need to use the wait function.

searchDocs.title: 4. Advanced Use​
searchDocs.content: You can also use this library in a more Unity styled way.  This method doesnt need WaitForSchema() to function.  You will have to make your own system to achieve this, this is commonly done through a module loader!  server init module   local ServerStorage = game:GetService("ServerStorage") local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local VluxySF = require(ReplicatedStorage.Packages.VluxySF) -- Reference to the folder/config of the actual sounds you want to use local soundFolder = ServerStorage:FindFirstChild("SOUNDS") local Orchestrator = {} Orchestrator.Init = function() VluxySF.Startup.InitServer(SOUNDS) end Orchestrator.Start = function() -- <--schema can be used here end return Orchestrator   client init module   local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local VluxySF = require(ReplicatedStorage.Packages.VluxySF) local Orchestrator = {} Orchestrator.Init = function() VluxySF.Startup.InitClient() <--yeilds until ready end Orchestrator.Start = function() -- <--schema can be used here end return Orchestrator   new client module   local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local VluxySF = require(ReplicatedStorage.Packages.VluxySF) local Orchestrator = {} Orchestrator.Start = function() -- <--schema can be used here end return Orchestrator   You can also use the Schema in sub modules instead of directly calling them from an Orchestrator if they run on Start or after.

searchDocs.title: InitServer​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the server. Server </> Startup.InitServer( soundFolder: Configuration -- The SOUNDS Configurartion. ) → () Injects the SOUNDS Configuration into the library and creates the Schema. INFO It compiles all the information into Folders, Configurations, Sounds, SoundGroups, and sounds names to Preload. The schema is locked once Start.InitServer runs. Meaning you cannot add new Sounds, SoundGroups, etc... to the library. DANGER This should only run once! local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local ServerStorage = game:GetService("ServerStorage") local VluxySF = require(ReplicatedStorage.VluxySF) local soundConfig = ServerStorage:FindFirstChild("SOUNDS") VluxySF.Startup.InitServer(soundConfig)

searchDocs.title: InitClient​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the client. Client This is a yielding function. When called, it will pause the Lua thread that called the function until a result is ready to be returned, without interrupting other scripts. Yields </> Startup.InitClient( timeout: number?-- Timeout for waiting on sounds to preload. ) → () Fetches the Schema from the server and preloads all sounds that require it. DANGER This should only run once! local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local VluxySF = require(ReplicatedStorage.VluxySF) local timeout = 4 VluxySF.Startup.InitClient(timeout)

searchDocs.title: IsInitialized​
searchDocs.content: </> Startup.IsInitialized() → boolean-- Is initialized? Checks if the sound schema has been initialized. print(VluxySF.Startup.IsInitialized())

searchDocs.title: WaitForSchema​
searchDocs.content: This is a yielding function. When called, it will pause the Lua thread that called the function until a result is ready to be returned, without interrupting other scripts. Yields </> Startup.WaitForSchema() → () Waits until the sound schema is initialized and ready for use. INFO If you know that your code will execute after the Schema is loaded then this is not needed. local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local VluxySF = require(ReplicatedStorage.VluxySF) VluxySF.Startup.WaitForSchema() --yields VluxySF.FetchPlayOnce("MySound")

searchDocs.title: ConnectForSchema​
searchDocs.content: </> Startup.ConnectForSchema( finished: () → ()-- Callback function to execute when the schema is created. ) → () → ()-- Cleanup function. Alternative to Startup.WaitForSchema if you do not want to pause the current block. local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local VluxySF = require(ReplicatedStorage.VluxySF) VluxySF.Startup.ConnectForSchema(function() VluxySF.FetchPlayOnce("MySound") end) --doesn't yield down here!

searchDocs.title: Basic Fetch​
searchDocs.content: This is the main function of this library! It reconstructs your sound on request.  local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local VluxySF = require(ReplicatedStorage.Packages.VluxySF) VluxySF.WaitForSchema() -- Wait until schema is loaded local sound = VluxySF.Fetch("LoFiBeat1") -- <-- Always returns a Sound Instance. --Do stuff here / adjust properties. sound:Play()   Note: "LoFiBeat1" is just an example Sound name in your SOUNDS Configuration. Note: If it fails to find your Sound a Blank Sound Instance is returned Instead.

searchDocs.title: Basic OneShot​
searchDocs.content: There is an alternative function for directly playing a sound if you just need to OneShot.  local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local VluxySF = require(ReplicatedStorage.Packages.VluxySF) VluxySF.WaitForSchema() -- Wait until schema is loaded. VluxySF.FetchPlayOnce("LoFiBeat1")

searchDocs.title: Alternative Fetch​
searchDocs.content: There is an alternative Fetch Function if you dont want a Blank Sound Instance on fail.  local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local VluxySF = require(ReplicatedStorage.Packages.VluxySF) VluxySF.WaitForSchema() -- Wait until schema is loaded. local sound = VluxySF.FetchStrict("LoFiBeat1") -- <-- Can return nil. if sound then --Do stuff here / adjust properties. sound:Play() end

searchDocs.title: Presets​
searchDocs.content: local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local VluxySF = require(ReplicatedStorage.Packages.VluxySF) VluxySF.WaitForSchema() --Wait until schema is loaded. local presets = VluxySF.Presets --Quicker way to use presets. --A combined preset as shown below lets you combine multiple presets together. local myPrest = presets.Combine( function(name: string) -- <-- you can also make your own presets. return function(sound: Sound) sound.Name = name .. math.random() end end presets.NaturalVolumeJitter(0.8, 0.12), presets.NaturalPlaybackJitter(0.7, 0.09), presets.Parent(workspace), ) VluxySF.FetchPlayOnce("RandomSound1", nil, nil, myPreset) task.delay(0.1, function() VluxySF.FetchPlayOnce("RandomSound1", nil, nil, myPreset) end) --Want this sound to have a similar preset but the parent is diffrent. local sound = VluxySF.Fetch("RandomSound1", nil, myPreset) sound.parent = workspace.CurrentCamera --This sound is diffrent but i want it to play similarly. local sound = VluxySF.Fetch("OtherRandomSound1", nil, myPreset)   Tip: You can make your own module of presets and use them just like shown here. Note: Combine presets get applied in order from first variable argument to last. Note: It is recommended that you only use presets to set properties and perform similar one time operations

searchDocs.title: SoundGroups​
searchDocs.content: Set the SoundGroup volumes at the start of a session. This can be done on server or client (client is recommended for user settings).  local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local VluxySF = require(ReplicatedStorage.Packages.VluxySF) VluxySF.WaitForSchema() local mainVolume = 0.8 local subVolumes = { ["GAME"] = 0.7, ["MUSIC"] = 0.55, ["SFX"] = 0.64, ... } local function onUpdate() --Do stuff here if you want, maybe clamp your values if you dont want it to be from 0, 10 VluxySF.Groups.SetVolumes(mainVolume, subVolumes) end --im too lazy to connect this to an event task.spawn(function() while true do onUpdate() task.wait(0.5) end end)   Tip: You can call this function whenever a slider or setting changes to update volumes live. Note: inputing the mainVolume is optional. So are the elements in the subVolumes table. Note: There is other functions you can use for manipulating your SoundGroups.

searchDocs.title: SoundDefinitions​
searchDocs.content: </> type SoundDefinitions = {[string]: SoundDefinition} A dictionary of sound names for fetching SoundDefinitions.

searchDocs.title: SoundDefinition​
searchDocs.content: </> interface SoundDefinition { Properties: SoundSerialized-- Properties of the sound. Effects: SoundChildrenSerialized-- Individual SoundEffects and their Properties. EffectsCount: number-- Amount of SoundEffects in the SoundDefinition. } Fully Serialized Sound.

searchDocs.title: SoundChildrenSerialized​
searchDocs.content: </> interface SoundChildrenSerialized { ChorusSoundEffect: ChorusSoundEffectSerialized? CompressorSoundEffect: CompressorSoundEffectSerialized? DistortionSoundEffect: DistortionSoundEffectSerialized? EchoSoundEffect: EchoSoundEffectSerialized? EqualizerSoundEffect: EqualizerSoundEffectSerialized? FlangeSoundEffect: FlangeSoundEffectSerialized? PitchShiftSoundEffect: PitchShiftSoundEffectSerialized? ReverbSoundEffect: ReverbSoundEffectSerialized? TremoloSoundEffect: TremoloSoundEffectSerialized? } Table of serialized SoundEffects for the SoundDefinition.

searchDocs.title: SoundSerialized​
searchDocs.content: </> interface SoundSerialized { AcousticSimulationEnabled: boolean AudioContent: string EmitterSize: number IsLoaded: boolean IsPaused: boolean IsPlaying: boolean isPlaying: boolean Looped: boolean LoopRegion: NumberRange MaxDistance: number MinDistance: number Pitch: number PlaybackLoudness: number PlaybackRegion: NumberRange PlaybackRegionsEnabled: boolean PlaybackSpeed: number Playing: boolean PlayOnRemove: boolean RollOffMaxDistance: number RollOffMinDistance: number RollOffMode: Enum.RollOffMode SoundGroup: string SoundId: string TimeLength: number TimePosition: number Volume: number Archivable: boolean Name: string Parent: Instance ClassName: string } Serialized Sound Instance.

searchDocs.title: ChorusSoundEffectSerialized​
searchDocs.content: </> interface ChorusSoundEffectSerialized { Depth: number Mix: number Rate: number Enabled: boolean Priority: number Archivable: boolean Name: string Parent: Instance ClassName: string } Serialized ChorusSoundEffect Instance.

searchDocs.title: CompressorSoundEffectSerialized​
searchDocs.content: </> interface CompressorSoundEffectSerialized { Attack: number GainMakeup: number Ratio: number Release: number Threshold: number Enabled: boolean Priority: number Archivable: boolean Name: string Parent: Instance ClassName: string } Serialized CompressorSoundEffect Instance.

searchDocs.title: DistortionSoundEffectSerialized​
searchDocs.content: </> interface DistortionSoundEffectSerialized { Level: number Enabled: boolean Priority: number Archivable: boolean Name: string Parent: Instance ClassName: string } Serialized DistortionSoundEffect Instance.

searchDocs.title: EchoSoundEffectSerialized​
searchDocs.content: </> interface EchoSoundEffectSerialized { Delay: number Feedback: number WetLevel: number DryLevel: number Enabled: boolean Priority: number Archivable: boolean Name: string Parent: Instance ClassName: string } Serialized EchoSoundEffect Instance.

searchDocs.title: EqualizerSoundEffectSerialized​
searchDocs.content: </> interface EqualizerSoundEffectSerialized { HighGain: number LowGain: number MidGain: number Enabled: boolean Priority: number Archivable: boolean Name: string Parent: Instance ClassName: string } Serialized EqualizerSoundEffect Instance.

searchDocs.title: FlangeSoundEffectSerialized​
searchDocs.content: </> interface FlangeSoundEffectSerialized { Depth: number Mix: number Rate: number Enabled: boolean Priority: number Archivable: boolean Name: string Parent: Instance ClassName: string } Serialized FlangeSoundEffect Instance.

searchDocs.title: PitchShiftSoundEffectSerialized​
searchDocs.content: </> interface PitchShiftSoundEffectSerialized { Octave: number Enabled: boolean Priority: number Archivable: boolean Name: string Parent: Instance ClassName: string } Serialized PitchShiftSoundEffect Instance.

searchDocs.title: ReverbSoundEffectSerialized​
searchDocs.content: </> interface ReverbSoundEffectSerialized { DecayTime: number Density: number Diffusion: number DryLevel: number WetLevel: number Enabled: boolean Priority: number Archivable: boolean Name: string Parent: Instance ClassName: string } Serialized ReverbSoundEffect Instance.

searchDocs.title: TremoloSoundEffectSerialized​
searchDocs.content: </> interface TremoloSoundEffectSerialized { Depth: number Duty: number Frequency: number Enabled: boolean Priority: number Archivable: boolean Name: string Parent: Instance ClassName: string } Serialized TremoloSoundEffect Instance.

searchDocs.title: Combine​
searchDocs.content: </> SoundPresets.Combine( ...: (Sound ) → ()-- Your Preset Closures to combine. ) → (Sound ) → ()-- Closure of the combined presets. Combines presets together into 1 reusable preset. INFO Presets are applied in sequence from first to last. local myCombinedPreset = SoundPresets.Combine( SoundPresets.NaturalPlaybackJitter(0.8, 0.1), SoundPresets.NaturalVolumeJitter(0.7, 0.15) ) local myCombinedPreset2 = SoundPresets.Combine( myCombinedPreset, SoundPresets.Parent(workspace.Sounds) ) local sound1 = VluxySF.Fetch("mySound1", nil, myCombinedPreset) local sound2 = VluxySF.Fetch("mySound2", nil, myCombinedPreset) local sound3 = VluxySF.Fetch("mySound3", nil, myCombinedPreset2) local sound4 = VluxySF.Fetch("mySound4", nil, myCombinedPreset2)

searchDocs.title: Parent​
searchDocs.content: </> SoundPresets.Parent( parent: Instance -- The parent to set the sound too. ) → (Sound ) → ()-- Closure of the preset. Sets the parent of a sound. local myPreset = SoundPresets.Parent(workspace.Sounds) local sound1 = VluxySF.Fetch("mySound1", nil, myPreset) local sound2 = VluxySF.Fetch("mySound2", nil, myPreset) local sound3 = VluxySF.Fetch("mySound3", nil, myPreset)

searchDocs.title: Volume​
searchDocs.content: </> SoundPresets.Volume( volume: number-- The volume to set the sound too. ) → (Sound ) → ()-- Closure of the preset. Sets the volume of a sound. local myPreset = SoundPresets.Volume(0.1) local sound1 = VluxySF.Fetch("mySound1", nil, myPreset) local sound2 = VluxySF.Fetch("mySound2", nil, myPreset) local sound3 = VluxySF.Fetch("mySound3", nil, myPreset)

searchDocs.title: PlaybackSpeed​
searchDocs.content: </> SoundPresets.PlaybackSpeed( speed: number-- The playback speed to set for the sound too. ) → (Sound ) → ()-- Closure of the preset. Sets the playback speed of a sound. local myPreset = SoundPresets.PlaybackSpeed(1.3) local sound1 = VluxySF.Fetch("mySound1", nil, myPreset) local sound2 = VluxySF.Fetch("mySound2", nil, myPreset) local sound3 = VluxySF.Fetch("mySound3", nil, myPreset)

searchDocs.title: NaturalVolumeJitter​
searchDocs.content: </> SoundPresets.NaturalVolumeJitter( center: number?,-- The central value of the jitter. spread: number?-- The spread of the jitter. ) → (Sound ) → ()-- Closure of the preset. Uses Jitter.UnitLog to apply a natural jitter to the volume of the sound. local myPreset = SoundPresets.NaturalVolumeJitter(0.8, 0.1) local sound1 = VluxySF.Fetch("mySound1", nil, myPreset) local sound2 = VluxySF.Fetch("mySound2", nil, myPreset) local sound3 = VluxySF.Fetch("mySound3", nil, myPreset)

searchDocs.title: NaturalPlaybackJitter​
searchDocs.content: </> SoundPresets.NaturalPlaybackJitter( center: number?,-- The central value of the jitter. spread: number?-- The spread of the jitter. ) → (Sound ) → ()-- Closure of the preset. Uses Jitter.UnitLog to apply a natural jitter to the playback speed of the sound. local myPreset = SoundPresets.NaturalPlaybackJitter(1, 0.1) local sound1 = VluxySF.Fetch("mySound1", nil, myPreset) local sound2 = VluxySF.Fetch("mySound2", nil, myPreset) local sound3 = VluxySF.Fetch("mySound3", nil, myPreset)

searchDocs.title: RandomVolumeJitter​
searchDocs.content: </> SoundPresets.RandomVolumeJitter( center: number?,-- The central value of the jitter. spread: number?-- The spread of the jitter. ) → (Sound ) → ()-- Closure of the preset. Uses Jitter.Default to apply a random jitter to the volume of the sound. local myPreset = SoundPresets.RandomVolumeJitter(0.8, 0.1) local sound1 = VluxySF.Fetch("mySound1", nil, myPreset) local sound2 = VluxySF.Fetch("mySound2", nil, myPreset) local sound3 = VluxySF.Fetch("mySound3", nil, myPreset)

searchDocs.title: RandomPlaybackJitter​
searchDocs.content: </> SoundPresets.RandomPlaybackJitter( center: number?,-- The central value of the jitter. spread: number?-- The spread of the jitter. ) → (Sound ) → ()-- Closure of the preset. Uses Jitter.Default to apply a random jitter to the playback speed of the sound. local myPreset = SoundPresets.RandomPlaybackJitter(1, 0.1) local sound1 = VluxySF.Fetch("mySound1", nil, myPreset) local sound2 = VluxySF.Fetch("mySound2", nil, myPreset) local sound3 = VluxySF.Fetch("mySound3", nil, myPreset)
